title: Market Brief — Sunday, April 5, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. H100 availability sits at just 24% (70/294), H200 at 30% (121/401), and B200 at 35% (144/409), all well below comfort. H100 spot dropped 2.7% day-over-day to $1.87/hr, the only notable move, suggesting mild demand softening at the legacy tier while next-gen silicon stays tight.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across every GPU class. H100 availability sits at just 24% (70/294), H200 at 30% (121/401), and B200 at 35% (144/409), all well below comfort. H100 spot dropped 2.7% day-over-day to $1.87/hr, the only notable move, suggesting mild demand softening at the legacy tier while next-gen silicon stays tight.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), H100 inference runs $6.83/M tokens and H200 hits $5.56/M. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $6.45/M tokens on H100, pure cash burn to buy market share. OpenAI's medium-tier GPT-5 Mini at $2/M output also bleeds on H100 and H200. Only premium-priced models like o3 Pro and Claude Opus 4.6 generate healthy margins. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued given Opus 4.6 delivers frontier capability at $25/M, a 69% discount. B200 capacity at $2.79/M raw cost is the undervalued play: lock in spot contracts now before availability tightens below 30%.
bullets: 24% H100 availability (70 of 294 GPUs) signals scarce legacy compute.
35% B200 availability (144 of 409 GPUs) is the loosest tier but still below neutral.
$1.87/hr H100 spot fell 2.7% day-over-day, the only GPU class to move.
$0.38/M DeepSeek V3.2 output pricing implies a loss of $6.45/M tokens on TCO-adjusted H100.
21.41 LLMflation index means API pricing has fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$80/M o3 Pro output costs 3.2x more than the next most expensive model.
